title: Ski resort slope graph (for trail-snap map-matching)
description: Explicit node-and-edge representation of a resort's slope + lift network. Purpose-built for GPS → run-segment snapping. One file per resort — see docs/TRAIL_SNAP_SCHEMA.md in the ski-platform repo for the why.

place_slug.description: Resort slug — must match the `place_slug` in the sibling catalog files.

version.description: Schema version. Bump when the shape changes in a breaking way.

id.description: Human-readable ID, `n-` prefix. Examples: n-summit, n-rainbow-fork, n-base-main. Must be unique within `nodes[]`.

alt_m.description: Altitude in meters, above sea level. Required — the algorithm uses this for uphill-vs-downhill disambiguation independent of `kind`.

kind.description: Advisory label for human readers. Degree of the node is what actually drives fork/merge detection.

notes.description: Optional author commentary — never read by the algorithm.

id.description: Human-readable ID, `e-` prefix. Examples: e-rainbow-upper, e-gondola-1. Unique within `edges[]`.

slope_id.description: The catalog slope ID this edge belongs to (matches an id in sibling `.slopes.json`). Null for lift + traverse edges.

kind.description: slope = downhill terrain counting as a run; lift = uphill transport (cuts runs); traverse = access road / cat track, part of the graph but not a run.

difficulty.description: Optional — mirrors the catalog's slope.difficulty enum.

from.description: Upstream node id. Directed — the skier enters this edge at `from` and leaves at `to`.

geometry.description: Polyline from `from` to `to`. First + last vertex coords must match the `from` / `to` node coords.

alt_m.description: Altitude in meters. Required per-vertex — no sparse altitudes. Sample from DEM if you don't know.

snapConfig.description: Optional per-resort algorithm tuning. Defaults live in code.

r_max_m.description: Candidate-edge radius around each GPS sample.

gap_bridge_s.description: Max GPS gap bridgeable without breaking a run.

conf_floor.description: Runs below this confidence are emitted as unsnapped.
